fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection logic on line 65 uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), which incorrectly treats intervals touching at their boundaries as non-overlapping. The condition should use strict inequalities (`<`, `>`) to correctly identify these adjacent intervals as conflicts.

spec.expr: (end__1 <= s or start__1 >= e) == (end__1 < s or start__1 > e)

constraints.intent: The buggy non-overlap condition (with <=, >=) should be equivalent to the correct strict condition (with <, >).
If a loop iteration completes without finding a conflict, the new interval must not touch or overlap the existing interval from that iteration.

spec.expr: all(interval[1] < s or interval[0] > e for s, e in existing)

constraints.intent: If the program proceeds to merge the new interval, it implies no overlaps (including adjacent ones) were found with any existing interval.
